abstract.para: For players who already know standard chess. This rulebook covers only the rules that differ from standard chess. Piece movement, turn order, castling, en passant, and promotion follow standard chess rules unless otherwise noted here.

chapter.title: Overview
chapter.para: Ultimate Chess is a variant of standard chess in which captures are never automatic. Instead, every attempted capture triggers a card battle — a round of War played with a standard deck. Luck, timing, and risk management layer on top of positional chess strategy.
This rulebook covers only the rules that differ from standard chess. Piece movement, turn order, castling, en passant, and promotion follow standard chess rules unless otherwise noted here.

note.para: Both players need a full standard deck of cards including both Jokers before the game begins. Alternatively, see for a card-free option.

chapter.title: Components

listitem.para: Standard chess set (board and all pieces)
Two standard 52-card decks with Jokers — one per player

chapter.para: Each player owns and shuffles their own deck. Both decks are independent; you always draw from and play cards from your own deck.

chapter.title: Core Rule: The Card Battle
chapter.para: Whenever a player attempts to capture an opponent's piece, both players resolve a card battle before the capture occurs. The outcome determines whether the capture succeeds.

section.title: How to Play a Card Battle

step.para: The attacking player declares the capture by moving their piece to the target square.
Both players simultaneously place 3 cards face-down, then 1 card face-up.
Compare the face-up cards. The higher card wins.

row.entry: Highest possible card. Trumps Aces. See .
Second highest. Beats all numbered cards.
Standard descending rank. King (K) high, 2 low.
Capture succeeds. Attacker's piece moves to the target square. Captured piece is removed.
Attacker's piece returns to its original square.
The attacking piece returns to its original square. The turn is consumed. Neither player loses a piece.
Capture succeeds AND the attacker takes one additional non-King piece of their choice.
ARMAGEDDON. Both pieces are removed AND each player removes three non-King pieces of their choice.

chapter.title: King Rules
chapter.para: Ultimate Chess has no check or checkmate. Kings are powerful, mobile, and mortal.

section.title: Kings Can Move Freely
section.para: A King may move into any square, including squares attacked by the opponent. There is no obligation to move out of attack, and no illegal moves based on King safety.

section.title: Capturing a King
section.para: Attacking a King requires winning a best-of-three card battle rather than a single round.

step.para: Play up to three card battle rounds.
The first player to win two rounds captures the King and wins the game.
If the battle ends 1–1 after two rounds, play a third and final round.

note.para: The Joker rules still apply within King battles. A Joker win in any single round counts as winning that round.

section.title: King Capturing a Non-King Piece
section.para: When a King attacks a non-King piece, it plays a standard single-round card battle.

important.para: If the King loses the card battle, the King is captured and the attacking player loses the game immediately. Moving a King into a capture attempt is a gamble.

section.title: Last Piece Standing
section.para: If a player has no non-King pieces remaining, Joker and Sniper rules change:

listitem.para: A Joker win counts as a standard card battle win only — no bonus piece is taken.
Armageddon — both Jokers — also resolves as a standard tie with no bonus pieces.

chapter.title: Sniper Rules
chapter.para: A Joker is called a Sniper in Ultimate Chess.

step.para: The attacker wins the card battle normally (the target piece is captured).
The attacker then selects any one non-King piece from the opponent's remaining army and removes it from the board without a card battle.

note.para: The bonus piece removal is immediate and requires no additional card play. The attacker chooses freely.

section.title: Armageddon — Both Players Play a Sniper
section.para: If both players play a Joker as their face-up card in the same round, Armageddon is triggered.

step.para: Both the attacking piece and the defending piece are immediately removed from the board.
Each player then selects and removes three non-King pieces from the opponent's army, without card battles.
Both players make their selections simultaneously — do not wait for your opponent to choose first.

important.para: Armageddon can dramatically change the board state. If either player has fewer than three non-King pieces remaining, they remove as many as they have.

chapter.title: Win Conditions
chapter.para: A player wins when any of the following occurs:

listitem.para: The opponent's King is captured in a King card battle (best of three).
The opponent's King attempts to capture a piece, loses the card battle, and is captured.

chapter.para: There is no checkmate, stalemate, or draw by repetition in Ultimate Chess. The game ends only when a King is captured.

note.para: Standard chess draw conditions — insufficient material, fifty-move rule, and mutual agreement — may be adopted by house rule if both players agree before the game begins.

chapter.title: Dice Variant
chapter.para: If playing cards are unavailable, use dice to resolve captures.

section.title: Standard Dice
section.para: Each player rolls one six-sided die per capture attempt. Higher roll wins. Reroll ties.

listitem.para: Rolling doubles on two dice counts as a Sniper win — apply .

section.title: Custom Dice (D&D Dice)
section.para: Players may agree on custom battle rules using polyhedral dice (D4, D6, D8, D10, D12, D20). Establish house rules before the game begins, including:

listitem.para: Which die or dice each player rolls
What result counts as a Sniper equivalent

note.para: Custom dice rules should be written down and agreed upon before the game. The same rules must apply equally to both players.

row.entry: Play 3 face-down + 1 face-up. Higher card wins.
Attacker's piece is removed. Defender's piece stays.
No pieces move or are removed. Turn consumed.
Capture succeeds + take one bonus non-King piece.
Armageddon: both pieces removed + each player removes 3 more non-King pieces.
Best of three card battles. First to two wins captures the King.
King is captured. Attacking player loses immediately.
